topic.what: Federated learning for hardware assurance using deep learning segmentation on SEM images
topic.how: Applied FedAvg algorithm with U-Net for IC reverse engineering segmentation; demonstrated gradient inversion attack vulnerability
topic.why_matters: Addresses data privacy concern in collaborative hardware assurance while exposing critical privacy risks in FL that threaten sensitive semiconductor IP

characteristics.what_it_measures: Intersection-over-Union for SEM image segmentation accuracy
characteristics.context: FL 9 clients vs CL 1-client with 10 images

characteristics.what_it_measures: Mean squared error for pixel-wise segmentation accuracy
Structural similarity for segmentation mask fidelity
characteristics.context: FL underperforms CL on full data in structural metrics

characteristics.what_it_measures: Peak signal-to-noise ratio of reconstructed SEM images from gradients
characteristics.context: Average over 100 samples; demonstrates successful gradient inversion

characteristics.what_it_measures: Structural similarity of reconstructed vs original SEM images
characteristics.context: Low SSIM due to noise but images recognizable to human observers
FL 9 clients takes longer than equivalent CL

good_for: collaborative hardware assurance across competing stakeholders
improving segmentation model performance with distributed SEM data
proof-of-concept for FL in semiconductor analysis
not_for: production deployment without additional privacy defenses
requires: REFICS dataset or similar SEM image datasets

novelty: First application of FL to hardware assurance using deep learning for segmentation on SEM images
Novel GIA loss function combining MSE and Cosine Similarity for segmentation tasks
Demonstration that FL alone cannot protect privacy - SEM images recoverable from gradients
Analysis of GIA under realistic constraints: no ground truth mask, high resolution 256x256, complex U-Net model
open_problems: Developing effective defenses against gradient inversion attacks in FL for hardware security
Applying GIA to transformer/language/multimodal models in hardware assurance
Evaluating FL privacy risks under non-iid data distributions
Combining FL with differential privacy, GAN, or secure aggregation for hardware security
Exploring other attacks: model stealing, membership inference, model poisoning in hardware contexts
